title: epipactis repens var. ophioides
intro: Epipactis repens var. ophioides, also known as the serpent's tongue orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to Europe, known for its unique, hooded flowers resembling a snake's tongue. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Epipactis repens var. ophioides are said to include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting tissue repair, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in infusions or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, but proper identification and dosing are crucial to avoid adverse reactions.
